config_template.notes: Supports in-place execution via output_tensor=input (e.g. ttnn.relu(x8, output_tensor=x8)). Output memory_config may differ from input (resharding ReLU: relu(x, memory_config=output_shard_config)). Layout: TILE_LAYOUT typical; works on interleaved DRAM/L1 and sharded (HEIGHT/WIDTH/BLOCK) inputs.

applicability_notes: fusion_notes: Pure elementwise unary; commonly absorbed as fused activation into preceding ttnn.linear/ttnn.matmul/ttnn.conv2d via activation='relu' in program_config, or fused into ttnn.add via add_and_apply_activation / unary post-op (residual add + relu pattern: ttnn.add(x, x_identity) -> ttnn.relu(x)).

placement_observations.size_descriptor: MLP hidden activation [batch, seq, hidden] after ttnn.linear (fc1)
placement_observations.source: call_site: hidden = ttnn.relu(hidden, memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG) between two DRAM-interleaved ttnn.linear calls

placement_observations.size_descriptor: classification-head intermediate after width-sharded linear (linear_1 -> relu -> linear_2)

memory_config.shard_spec_template: WIDTH_SHARDED inherited from producing ttnn.linear(memory_config=ttnn.L1_WIDTH_SHARDED_MEMORY_CONFIG); relu called with no memory_config, preserves input sharding

placement_observations.size_descriptor: large 5D activation reshaped to (N*D*H, W, C), tilized
placement_observations.source: call_site: ttnn.relu(x8, output_tensor=x8) after ttnn.to_layout(x7, ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT) — in-place to avoid extra allocation for large tensor

placement_observations.size_descriptor: conv1d sequence activation [batch, seq_len, channels] (TDNN/conv blocks)
placement_observations.source: call_site: y = ttnn.relu(y, memory_config=mc_dram) after conv1d block / before ttnn.layer_norm

placement_observations.size_descriptor: SE-block bottleneck [batch, y_len, out1_ch] after ttnn.conv1d + reshape
placement_observations.source: call_site: y = ttnn.relu(y, memory_config=dram) following ttnn.reshape(y, (batch, y_len, out1_ch), memory_config=dram)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: Res2Net / attention-pooling NLC activation [batch, seq_len, channels]

memory_config.shard_spec_template: interleaved L1 (block operates with mc = ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG); relu called with no memory_config, inherits

placement_observations.source: call_site: a_tt = ttnn.relu(a_tt) inside Res2Net/attentive-stat-pooling block where mc = ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG

placement_observations.size_descriptor: sharded 4D activation (batch, C, H, W) flattened

memory_config.shard_spec_template: {SHARD_STRATEGY} sharded (in_sharded_mem_config), output kept in same shard config then converted via ttnn.to_memory_config(out, ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: wide-row tensors (1, W) with row_width > tile_size, e.g. (1,1032), (1,2048) — chunked across width

memory_config.shard_spec_template: output_shard_config may differ from input_shard_config (relu performs implicit reshard)

placement_observations.source: test: ttnn.relu(ttnn_input, memory_config=output_shard_config) with sharded input/output configs

placement_observations.size_descriptor: residual-add output, same shard spec as identity branch

memory_config.shard_spec_template: inherits x.memory_config() from preceding ttnn.add(x, x_identity, memory_config=x.memory_config()); shard shape/grid matched to x_identity via ttnn.reshard

placement_observations.source: call_site: x = ttnn.relu(x) after ttnn.add residual merge (ResNet-style block)
